title: sdk: typed ctx.halt() for honest early-exits (vs fake RUN_COMPLETED on halt:true return)
issueBody: When a process function returns { success: false, halt: true } from an early-exit path, the SDK currently treats this as a clean run completion. Operators see RUN_COMPLETED with a completionProof even though the process halted without doing the intended work. Proposed core fix: add typed ctx.halt(reason, payload?) for honest early exits, record RUN_HALTED instead of RUN_COMPLETED, report run:status state=halted with reason/payload, and make CLI behavior distinguish halted from completed. Legacy { halt: true } returns should migrate by warning and recording halted. Broad static analysis/output validation for resolver-style task outputs should be scoped as follow-up unless it can be made narrow and reliable.

comments.author: a5c-ai
comments.summary: Automated triage confirmed the root cause: normal process returns are always treated as successful completion. Recommended adding ctx.halt, RUN_HALTED, halted run:status, non-success CLI behavior, legacy halt:true migration warning, regression tests, and docs. It explicitly warned that broad static analysis should be follow-up unless small and reliable.

acceptanceCriteria: ctx.halt(reason: string, payload?: object) is exposed on ProcessContext as a typed early-exit API.
return ctx.halt('phase-0', { reason: 'invalid-input' }) records RUN_HALTED, not RUN_COMPLETED.
run:status --json reports state='halted', reason, payload, and completionProof=null for halted runs.
run:iterate/CLI behavior distinguishes halted from completed and returns non-zero for halted terminal runs.
Normal successful returns still record RUN_COMPLETED and expose completionProof.
Legacy { success: false, halt: true } returns record RUN_HALTED, emit a one-line deprecation warning, and do not fake completion.
Terminal replay of halted runs is idempotent and does not re-execute process code.
Documentation explains RUN_COMPLETED, RUN_HALTED, and RUN_FAILED process exit conventions.
nonGoals: Do not implement broad static analysis of arbitrary process JavaScript in the core halt lifecycle fix.
Do not remove legacy { halt: true } support immediately; keep a migration path.
Do not change completionProof behavior for genuinely completed runs.
Do not change failed/process-error semantics except where shared terminal-state helpers need to recognize halted.
qualityCommands: npm exec --workspace=@a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk -- vitest run src/cli/__tests__/cliRuns.test.ts src/harness/__tests__/harness.test.ts src/cli/commands/session/__tests__/iterationMessage.test.ts
